title: Lepidaploa tovarensis
intro: Lepidaploa tovarensis, also known as Tovarensis, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lepidaploa tovarensis include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in traditional medicine to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
